root.short.other: API request purification and response analysis tool

root.long.other:
apix - API request purification and response analysis tool

Supports minimizing HTTP requests, extracting response schemas, and generating condensed JSON data.
Uses Go HTTP client by default, no curl installation required.

Main commands:
  pure    Minimize HTTP requests by removing redundant parameters
  mini    Get condensed JSON response data
  schema  Get JSON Schema structure of response
  clean   Clean HTTP response cache

Output formats: json, yaml, toml

Quick start:
  # Minimize request - auto-remove headers, query, body, cookie that don't affect response
  apix pure curl https://api.example.com -H "Authorization: Bearer token"

  # Serial mode - test parameters one by one (for parameters with dependencies)
  apix pure -s curl https://api.example.com -H "X-A: 1" -H "X-B: 2"

  # Custom match condition - based on status code or JSON fields
  apix pure -m expr --match-expr "status==200 && $.code==0" curl https://api.example.com

  # Get condensed response (long strings truncated, arrays keep only first/last)
  apix mini curl https://api.example.com

  # Get response schema
  apix schema curl https://api.example.com

  # Process JSON from stdin
  echo '{"key":"value"}' | apix mini
  cat data.json | apix schema

  # Clean cache
  apix clean

Use "apix [command] --help" for more information about a command.

pure.short.other: Minimize HTTP request and output minimal request

pure.long.other:
Execute curl request and purify, output minimized request.

Automatically test and remove headers, query, body, cookie that don't affect response structure.
Supports multiple output formats: curl (default), json, http.

Expression matching (-m expr):
  Use --match-expr to specify match conditions, supports the following variables:
    status              HTTP status code (int)
    content_length      Response body length (int)
    line_count          Response body line count (int)
    headers             Response headers map (case-insensitive access)
    body                Response body JSON data (map/any)
    $.field             Shortcut for body.field

  Operators: == != < > <= >= && || ! contains()

  Examples:
    status == 200
    status == 200 && $.code == 0
    headers['content-type'].contains('json')
    status == 200 && line_count < 50
    $.data != nil

pure.example.other:
# Default output curl command
  apix pure curl https://httpbin.org/get

  # Serial mode (test one by one, for parameters with dependencies)
  apix pure -s curl https://httpbin.org/get -H "X-A: 1" -H "X-B: 2"

  # Control parallelism (reduce concurrency to avoid rate limiting)
  apix pure --parallel 4 curl https://httpbin.org/get

  # Output JSON format (similar to HAR)
  apix pure -j curl https://httpbin.org/get

  # Output HTTP protocol format
  apix pure -t curl https://httpbin.org/get

  # Match status code (default is structure)
  apix pure -m status curl https://httpbin.org/get

  # Expression match: status 200 and JSON code equals 0
  apix pure -m expr --match-expr "status==200 && $.code==0" curl https://api.example.com

  # Expression match: Content-Type contains json (headers access is case-insensitive)
  apix pure -m expr --match-expr "headers['content-type'].contains('json')" curl https://httpbin.org/get

  # POST form data minimize
  apix pure curl -X POST -d "a=1&b=2&c=3" https://httpbin.org/post

  # Cookie minimize (test each cookie individually)
  apix pure curl https://httpbin.org/cookies -b "session=abc;tracking=xyz"

  # Custom DNS resolution HOST:PORT:IP
  apix pure curl --resolve "www.example.org:443:127.0.0.1" https://www.example.org

  # Use system curl and disable cache
  apix pure -c --no-cache curl https://httpbin.org/get

mini.long.other:
Get condensed JSON data.

Long strings are automatically truncated (>30 chars), arrays keep only first and last elements.

Usage:
  apix mini [args] curl [curl args...]  # From HTTP
  cat data.json | apix mini [args]       # From stdin

mini.example.other:
# Get condensed response from HTTP
  apix mini curl https://api.example.com

  # Output YAML format
  apix mini -o yaml curl https://api.example.com

  # Read JSON from stdin
  echo '{"key":"value"}' | apix mini
  cat data.json | apix mini -p

schema.short.other: Get JSON Schema structure of response

schema.long.other:
Get JSON data structure (JSON Schema) of response.

Returns JSON Schema format by default, supports reading from HTTP or stdin.

Usage:
  apix schema [args] curl [curl args...]  # From HTTP
  cat data.json | apix schema [args]       # From stdin

schema.example.other:
# Get response schema from HTTP
  apix schema curl https://api.example.com

  # Output YAML format schema
  apix schema -o yaml curl https://api.example.com

  # Generate schema from stdin
  echo '{"id":1,"name":"test"}' | apix schema -p

clean.long.other:
Clean all HTTP response cache files stored in temporary directory.

Cache is used to accelerate repeated HTTP requests and avoid requesting the same URL multiple times during minimization.
Run this command if you suspect cache data is expired or has issues.

flag.serial.other: Use serial mode (minimize one by one)

usage.mini.other:
apix mini [args] curl [curl args...]
  cat data.json | apix mini [args]

usage.schema.other:
apix schema [args] curl [curl args...]
  cat data.json | apix schema [args]
